title: Ballard Power Systems Q3 2021 Earnings Call Summary
text: Ballard announced that its fuel cell technology has powered vehicles over 100 million cumulative kilometers globally, positioning the company as a significant leader. Europe showed strong momentum with nearly 160 fuel cell buses in service across over 25 cities. North America, particularly California, expected nearly 80 additional buses on roads soon. China operations faced policy uncertainty and slower adoption in 2021. CEO Randy MacEwen emphasized a shift toward integrated powertrain solutions through partnerships with Hexagon Purus, Forsee Power, and QUANTRON. Recent strategic partnerships aim to simplify fuel cell vehicle adoption for OEM customers. Beyond traditional bus markets, Ballard highlighted traction in Marine (first fuel cell ferry delivery expected year-end), Rail (hydrogen-powered passenger trains with Talgo and CP Rail), and Stationary Power (90% revenue increase year-over-year in Q3). The company expects 2022 to mark the start of a long-term revenue ramp with strong customer engagement across all verticals, though management declined to provide specific guidance on profitability timing.
